tldr: Adyen and Modern Treasury solve different problems: Adyen accepts card and wallet payments globally, Modern Treasury orchestrates US bank transfers (ACH, wire, RTP, FedNow) with a real-time ledger. A 'migration' usually means moving the bank-money-movement use case to Modern Treasury while keeping (or replacing separately) card acceptance.
why_switch: Native ACH, wire, RTP, and FedNow APIs
Better fit for high-volume bank transfers
what_to_export: Internal ledger entries tied to Adyen events
export_method: Identify which money flows you actually want to move: card acceptance stays on Adyen (or another card PSP), while bank-money-movement (ACH, wire, RTP, FedNow) moves to Modern Treasury. Export payout schedules, counterparty bank details, reconciliation reports, and settlement files from Adyen using its standard reporting tools. Document any internal ledger or accounting entries that depend on Adyen webhooks so they can be repointed. Save webhook signing secrets and payload samples. Treat finance reports as the source of truth across the cutover; this is finance-grade data and must remain auditable.
import_method: Onboard with Modern Treasury, including bank partnership setup, since they require integration with your originating bank. Configure counterparties and payment orders for ACH, wire, RTP, and FedNow as needed. Wire your ledger system to Modern Treasury's ledgering API or use their built-in real-time ledger to replace bespoke double-entry code. Re-implement webhook handlers against their event format. Run small-value test transactions per rail in sandbox, then production, before moving real volume. Continue running Adyen for card acceptance unless you are also replacing that with another PSP separately.

concept_mapping.target: Out of scope; keep on Adyen or another PSP

effort: 2-6 months depending on bank partnership and ledger integration scope
gotchas: Modern Treasury is not a card processor; you still need a PSP for card acceptance
US bank focus — limited fit for global money movement
Bank partnership and onboarding work can dominate the timeline
Webhook formats and signature verification are different and must be re-implemented
Real-time rails (RTP/FedNow) have eligibility and bank-side requirements
Refunds and disputes on existing Adyen transactions still flow through Adyen

email.subject: Moving bank-payment ops from Adyen to Modern Treasury — kickoff plan
email.body:
Hi team,

We are migrating our US bank-payment operations from Adyen to Modern Treasury. Card acceptance will stay where it is for now. The full program will run beyond 14 days because of bank onboarding, but here is the kickoff plan.

Days 1-3: scope which money flows move (ACH, wire, RTP, FedNow) and confirm bank partnership.
Days 4-7: open Modern Treasury account, configure counterparties, start ledger integration design.
Days 8-11: build sandbox flows, send test transactions on each rail.
Days 12-14: lock in production rollout plan and reconciliation handoff.

We will keep Adyen running for cards and for any in-flight settlements.

Thanks,
Your customer
